You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.1714 (commit: 7c30e125).

## Issue to Resolve

Update to Anki 23.12 backend
The Anki update includes some significant changes to the FSRS algorithm. So, for best results, all reviews should be done on apps with the same codebase.

(There are no "breaking" changes, but still ...)

Now that the 23.12 release is public, the backend code of AnkiDroid should be updated as soon as possible.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I/we get notifications for anki releases, we are aware of them for what it's worth
I was almost certain that you guys get notifications for the releases, but I still created this issue because the 23.12 update changes the scheduler code, which means that doing reviews on apps with different backend versions can lead to some unwanted behaviour. So, quickly updating to this version is somewhat more important.

After this release, for several months, there are no expected changes to the FSRS scheduler code. So, I would not urge for a very rapid update to the new backend version for those releases.
Also, 23.12 introduced some important fixes. From release notes:

- When cards are rescheduled multiple times without reviews, extra entries will not be added to the review log.
- Fix some bugs in sorting by FSRS retrievability.
- Recompute FSRS memory state when cards moved into different deck.
- Added a new v3 option to limit child decks by their parent, when clicking on the child.
- Fix filtered deck ordering of intraday learning cards and reviews
- Fix relative overdueness order error in filtered deck.
The new backend can't be simply dropped in. Some changes will need to be made to AnkiDroid:

- The filtered deck screen will need to support customizing the separate preview delays for again/hard & good buttons
- The auto advance feature will need to switch to using the settings in the current card's deck options
> The new backend can't be simply dropped in.

Yes, I agree. All that I have to say is this update should take a slightly higher priority than normal.
Another issue I didn't mention above: Anki displays various options when importing an .apkg file, which AnkiDroid is currently not doing. As a temporary workaround to get things to compile when switching to the 23.12 backend, you can hard-code the argument change:

```diff
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
index 07ec516536..bb272bd17b 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 package com.ichi2.libanki

 import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
+import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions
 import anki.search.SearchNode
 import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

@@ -107,7 +108,13 @@ fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
     limit: ExportLimit,
     legacy: Boolean = true
 ) {
-    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
+    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
+        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
+        this.withMedia = withMedia
+        this.legacy = legacy
+        this.withDeckConfigs = true
+    }
+    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
 }

 fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
diff --git a/build.gradle b/build.gradle
index aeafc762f8..2057d8a32f 100644
--- a/build.gradle
+++ b/build.gradle
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ buildscript {
     ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.21'
     ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
     ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
-    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.32-anki23.10.1'
+    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12'
     ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
     ext.junit_version = '5.10.1'
     ext.coroutines_version = '1.7.3'
```

A proper fix will mean using the import-anki-package.html page. Currently, for some unknown reason, it is not being included in the backend builds, and it could use the help of someone knowledgeable in Gradle to debug.

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android-Backend/pull/339

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/card_template.html`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/BackendImporting.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiPackageImporterFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiServer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/PostRequestHandler.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerViewModel.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt`
- `build.gradle`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

42→        val changes = OpChangesOnly.parseFrom(output)
43→        undoableOp { changes }
44→        output
45→    }
46→}
47→
48→/**
49→ * Css to hide the "Show" button from the final backend import page. As the user could import a lot
50→ * of notes, on pressing "Show" the native CardBrowser would be called with a search query
51→ * comprising of all the notes ids. This would result in a crash or very slow behavior in the
52→ * CardBrowser.
53→ *
54→ * NOTE: this should be used only with [android.webkit.WebView.evaluateJavascript].
55→ */
56→val hideShowButtonCss = """
57→    javascript:(
58→        function() {
59→            var hideShowButtonStyle = '.desktop-only { display: none !important; }';
60→            var newStyle = document.createElement('style');                    
61→            newStyle.appendChild(document.createTextNode(hideShowButtonStyle));
62→            document.head.appendChild(newStyle);       
63→        }
64→    )()
65→""".trimIndent()
66→
67→/**
68→ * Calls the native [CardBrowser] to display the results of the search query constructed from the
69→ * input. This method will always return the received input.
70→ */
71→suspend fun FragmentActivity.searchInBrowser(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
72→    val searchString = withCol { buildSearchString(input) }
73→    val starterIntent = Intent(this, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
74→        putExtra("search_query", searchString)
75→        putExtra("all_decks", true)
76→    }
77→    startActivity(starterIntent)
78→    return input
79→}

 18→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19→
 20→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25→
 26→const val PORT = 0
 27→// const val PORT = 40001
 28→
 29→// local debugging:
 30→// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31→
 32→open class AnkiServer(
 33→    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34→) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35→
 36→    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37→        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41→    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42→    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43→
 44→    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45→        val uri = session.uri
 46→        val mime = getMimeFromUri(uri)
 47→
 48→        if (session.method == Method.GET) {
 49→            val resourcePath = "web$uri"
 50→            val stream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream(resourcePath)
 51→            Timber.d("GET: Requested %s (%s), found? %b", uri, resourcePath, stream != null)
 52→            return newChunkedResponse(Response.Status.OK, mime, stream)
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        if (session.method == Method.POST) {
 56→            Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 57→            val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 58→            return buildResponse {
 59→                postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→        return newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun buildResponse(
 66→        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 67→    ): Response {
 68→        return try {
 69→            val data = runBlocking {
 70→                block()
 71→            }
 72→            newChunkedResponse(data)
 73→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 74→            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 75→        }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    companion object {
 79→        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 80→
 81→        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 82→        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 83→        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 84→
 85→        fun getMimeFromUri(uri: String): String {
 86→            return when (uri.substringAfterLast(".")) {
 87→                "ico" -> "image/x-icon"
 88→                "css" -> "text/css"
 89→                "js" -> "text/javascript"
 90→                "html" -> "text/html"
 91→                else -> "application/binary"
 92→            }
 93→        }
 94→
 95→        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 96→            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 97→            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 98→            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 99→            return bytes
100→        }
101→
102→        fun newChunkedResponse(
103→            data: ByteArray?,
104→            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
105→            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
106→        ): Response {
107→            return if (data == null) {
108→                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
109→            } else {
110→                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
111→            }
112→        }
113→    }
114→}

142→        // How does it perform? https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dadsv5 (vs previous Standard_DS2_v2 https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dv2---general-compute)
143→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = 4
144→    } else {
145→        // Use 50% of cores to account for SMT which doesn't help this workload
146→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = Runtime.runtime.availableProcessors().intdiv(2) ?: 1
147→    }

 23→/**
 24→ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 25→ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 26→ * and this routine will return false.
 27→ *
 28→ * There must not be an active transaction.
 29→ *
 30→ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 31→ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 32→ * to check for success.
 33→ *
 34→ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 35→ *
 36→ */
 37→fun Collection.createBackup(
 38→    backupFolder: String,
 39→    force: Boolean,
 40→    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 41→): Boolean {
 42→    return backend.createBackup(
 43→        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 44→        force = force,
 45→        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 46→    )
 47→}
 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 51→ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 52→ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 53→ */
 54→fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 55→    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
 56→}

 58→/**
 59→ * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 60→ * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 61→ * */
 62→fun importCollectionPackage(
 63→    backend: Backend,
 64→    colPath: String,
 65→    colpkgPath: String
 66→) {
 67→    backend.importCollectionPackage(
 68→        colPath = colPath,
 69→        backupPath = colpkgPath,
 70→        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
 71→        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
 72→    )
 73→}

 75→/**
 76→ * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 77→ * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 78→ * create, but older clients can not read it.
 79→ */
 80→fun Collection.exportCollectionPackage(
 81→    outPath: String,
 82→    includeMedia: Boolean,
 83→    legacy: Boolean = true
 84→) {
 85→    close(forFullSync = true)
 86→    backend.exportCollectionPackage(
 87→        outPath = outPath,
 88→        includeMedia = includeMedia,
 89→        legacy = legacy
 90→    )
 91→    reopen()
 92→}

48→/**

50→ * of notes, on pressing "Show" the native CardBrowser would be called with a search query
51→ * comprising of all the notes ids. This would result in a crash or very slow behavior in the

56→val hideShowButtonCss = """
57→    javascript:(

59→            var hideShowButtonStyle = '.desktop-only { display: none !important; }';
60→            var newStyle = document.createElement('style');                    
61→            newStyle.appendChild(document.createTextNode(hideShowButtonStyle));
62→            document.head.appendChild(newStyle);

67→/**

69→ * input. This method will always return the received input.

71→suspend fun FragmentActivity.searchInBrowser(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
72→    val searchString = withCol { buildSearchString(input) }
73→    val starterIntent = Intent(this, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
74→        putExtra("search_query", searchString)
75→        putExtra("all_decks", true)
76→    }
77→    startActivity(starterIntent)
78→    return input

26→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
27→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
28→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
29→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
30→        .setDomain(domain)
31→        .addPathHandler("/web/") { path: String ->
32→            val inputStream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream("web/$path")
33→            WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
34→        }
35→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
36→            try {
37→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40→            } catch (e: Exception) {
41→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42→                null
43→            }
44→        }
45→        .build()
46→}

27→class AnkiPackageImporterFragment : PageFragment() {
28→    override val title: String
29→        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu_import)
30→    override val pageName: String
31→        get() = "import-page"
32→    override lateinit var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
33→    override var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient = PageChromeClient()
34→    private lateinit var backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
35→
36→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
37→        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
38→        backCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
39→            override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
40→                CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
41→                // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
42→                remove()
43→            }
44→        }
45→        val path = arguments?.getString(ARG_FILE_PATH)
46→            ?: throw IllegalStateException("No path provided for apkg package to import")
47→        webViewClient = AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(path, backCallback)
48→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
49→        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
50→    }
51→
52→    class AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(
53→        private val path: String,
54→        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
55→    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
56→        /**
57→         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
58→         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
59→         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
60→         */
61→        private var isDone = false
62→
63→        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
64→            val params = """{ type: "json_file", path: "$path"}"""
65→            // https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/main/ts/import-page/index.ts
66→            view!!.evaluateJavascript("anki.setupImportPage($params);$hideShowButtonCss;") {
67→                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
68→            }
69→        }
70→
71→        override fun onLoadResource(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
72→            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
73→            backCallback.isEnabled = when {
74→                url == null -> false
75→                url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
76→                url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
77→                    isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
78→                    false
79→                }
80→                else -> false
81→            }
82→        }
83→    }
84→
85→    companion object {
86→        private const val ARG_FILE_PATH = "arg_file_path"
87→
88→        fun getIntent(context: Context, filePath: String): Intent {
89→            val args = bundleOf(ARG_FILE_PATH to filePath)
90→            return SingleFragmentActivity.getIntent(context, AnkiPackageImporterFragment::class, args)
91→        }
92→    }
93→}

 18→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19→
 20→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25→
 26→const val PORT = 0
 27→// const val PORT = 40001
 28→
 29→// local debugging:
 30→// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31→
 32→open class AnkiServer(
 33→    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34→) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35→
 36→    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37→        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41→    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42→    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43→
 44→    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45→        val uri = session.uri
 46→        val mime = getMimeFromUri(uri)
 47→
 48→        if (session.method == Method.GET) {
 49→            val resourcePath = "web$uri"
 50→            val stream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream(resourcePath)
 51→            Timber.d("GET: Requested %s (%s), found? %b", uri, resourcePath, stream != null)
 52→            return newChunkedResponse(Response.Status.OK, mime, stream)
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        if (session.method == Method.POST) {
 56→            Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 57→            val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 58→            return buildResponse {
 59→                postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→        return newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun buildResponse(
 66→        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 67→    ): Response {
 68→        return try {
 69→            val data = runBlocking {
 70→                block()
 71→            }
 72→            newChunkedResponse(data)
 73→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 74→            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 75→        }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    companion object {
 79→        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 80→
 81→        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 82→        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 83→        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 84→
 85→        fun getMimeFromUri(uri: String): String {
 86→            return when (uri.substringAfterLast(".")) {
 87→                "ico" -> "image/x-icon"
 88→                "css" -> "text/css"
 89→                "js" -> "text/javascript"
 90→                "html" -> "text/html"
 91→                else -> "application/binary"
 92→            }
 93→        }
 94→
 95→        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 96→            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 97→            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 98→            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 99→            return bytes
100→        }
101→
102→        fun newChunkedResponse(
103→            data: ByteArray?,
104→            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
105→            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
106→        ): Response {
107→            return if (data == null) {
108→                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
109→            } else {
110→                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
111→            }
112→        }
113→    }
114→}
